evals.prompt: Group our search queries into topics so I can see the themes.
evals.expected_output: Should use report id query-clusters to group related Search Console queries so repeated demand themes are easier to review. Should follow the discovery flow: seo_list_reports with category opportunities, seo_describe_report with id query-clusters, then seo_run_report with the required site. Should read the summary cluster and query counts, clicks, impressions, threshold, limit, and brand filtering first, then per-cluster member queries, pages, demand, and performance. Should clarify token overlap does not prove shared intent.
assertions: Uses report id query-clusters for the query grouping
Follows seo_list_reports, seo_describe_report, seo_run_report in order
Reads the summary counts, threshold, limit, and brand filtering first
Reads per-cluster member queries, pages, demand, and performance
States token overlap does not prove shared intent

evals.prompt: Run query clusters for sc-domain:example.com, at least 100 impressions, top 40 clusters, scope /blog/.
evals.expected_output: Should run report id query-clusters with site sc-domain:example.com, minImpressions 100, limit 40, and scope /blog/. Should read per-cluster member queries, pages, and demand, then each cluster recommendation. Should note the threshold and cap in the summary bound the conclusion, and use only parameters defined in the schema.
assertions: Runs report id query-clusters with minImpressions 100 and limit 40
Sets scope to /blog/ as requested
Reads per-cluster member queries, pages, and demand
Reads each cluster recommendation
Uses only parameters defined in the report schema

evals.prompt: Each cluster should become a new page, and this proves those existing URLs are competing for the same thing.
evals.expected_output: Should decline both conclusions. Should state token overlap does not prove shared intent, justify a new page, or establish that current URLs compete. Should recommend checking whether a cluster maps to one intent before changing section structure or navigation, and route to cannibalisation to check URL overlap when competition is the real question.
assertions: Declines to turn each cluster into a new page
States token overlap does not prove shared intent or justify a new page
States token overlap does not establish that current URLs compete
Recommends checking whether a cluster maps to one intent before restructuring
Routes to report id cannibalisation to check URL overlap

evals.prompt: Queries under the threshold basically do not exist, and these page-two clusters will just pick up clicks from better CTR.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse both readings. Should state queries below the threshold or outside the cap are outside the conclusion, not absent demand, and that page-two clusters do not claim a CTR-only click lift. Should read the summary threshold, limit, and brand filtering to see what was excluded, and keep excluded and observed states distinct.
assertions: Refuses to treat below-threshold queries as absent demand
States queries below the threshold or outside the cap are outside the conclusion, not absent demand
States page-two clusters do not claim a CTR-only click lift
Reads the summary threshold, limit, and brand filtering to see what was excluded
Keeps excluded and observed states distinct
